events.title: US and Iran Reportedly Reach 'Great Settlement'
events.desc: President Donald Trump announced that the US and Iran have reached a "great settlement" to end the ongoing war. He indicated that the deal, which could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, is conceptual but expected to be finalized and signed soon, possibly over the weekend in Europe. Iran's foreign ministry, however, stated that no final decision has been made and that the country will not compromise on its "red lines."

events.title: US Cancels Planned Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: President Donald Trump announced the cancellation of scheduled strikes and bombings against Iran, stating that discussions had reached the highest level of Iranian leadership and were approved. This decision came after earlier threats of further bombings and an intention to seize the oil export hub Kharg Island.

events.title: Iran-born Engineer Granted Bail Ahead of Drone Strike Trial
events.desc: An Iranian-born engineer, Mahdi Sadeghi, has been granted bail by a U.S. judge ahead of his trial. Sadeghi faces charges related to illegally procuring technology for navigation systems used in Iran's military drones, which were allegedly involved in a deadly drone attack on a U.S. military base in Jordan in 2024.

events.title: US Plans to Deport Iranians to Central African Republic
events.desc: The Trump administration reportedly plans to deport a number of Iranians and other migrants to the Central African Republic. Among those facing deportation are two women who, according to their lawyer, risk torture and persecution if returned to Iran due to their religious conversion and activism.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: The US and Iran engaged in retaliatory strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. Iran claimed to have targeted American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, with Bahrain's capital reporting damage overnight.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors were killed when the U.S. military attacked a Palau-flagged oil tanker on Wednesday. The strike occurred after the tanker allegedly failed to comply with directions from U.S. forces.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a U.S. military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed a response. Subsequently, U.S. forces conducted strikes against Iran.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Hit Lebanese City of Tyre Despite Iranian Warning
events.desc: Israeli air strikes targeted the Lebanese city of Tyre, days after Iran had warned Israel to cease attacks on its ally Hezbollah. The strikes occurred despite Iran's warning that it might resume hostilities if attacks continued.

events.title: Crew's Distress Call After Ship Hit by US Missile
events.desc: The crew of a sanctioned oil tanker issued a distress call after the vessel was struck by a missile fired from a U.S. fighter jet off the coast of Oman. The missile strike resulted in the rescue of the two-person crew.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Days After US-Brokered Truce
events.desc: Israel launched strikes on a Beirut suburb, reportedly in response to Hezbollah firing rockets into Israeli territory. This action follows a U.S.-brokered truce aimed at de-escalating tensions in the region.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of at least 17 people. Nine casualties were reported in the town of Tayr Debba following a series of attacks.

events.title: Iran and Israel Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel have announced a temporary pause in their strikes, with leaders stating they are holding fire 'at the moment.' However, both nations have warned of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached again, indicating continued high tensions.

events.title: Strike, Counterstrike, Repeat in Lebanon
events.desc: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has intensified, leading to direct strikes exchanged between Israel and Iran on June 7th. This escalation complicates U.S.-Iranian negotiations, as Iran has linked progress to an end in Israeli actions against Hezbollah.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Despite a fragile ceasefire, satellite imagery reveals significant damage in southern Lebanon. At least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli Defense Forces' 'Yellow Line' have been heavily damaged or flattened, indicating ongoing destruction.

events.title: Efforts to Destroy Iran's Police Infrastructure
events.desc: Efforts to 'make Iran ungovernable' have targeted police stations and paramilitary Basij infrastructure. At least 80 such sites have been damaged or destroyed in the initial weeks of the U.S. and Israeli conflict with Iran, aiming to degrade the regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: Pentagon's AI Edge Being Distilled Away
events.desc: The Pentagon's reliance on publicly released frontier AI models creates a significant risk, as adversaries can harvest the logic of these models, undermining the U.S. military's AI-first approach. This compromises the integrity of advanced systems tied to commercial AI developers.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been found circulating online. The exposed credentials reveal basic security vulnerabilities in ministries handling classified information, potentially compromising sensitive data.
